name: Nebraska SNAP Food Assistance
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income Nebraska households through the ACCESSNebraska system.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Nebraska Medicaid
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income Nebraskans including Heritage Health managed care for most enrollees.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 218% FPL; pregnant women up to 194% FPL

name: Nebraska Department of Labor - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to Nebraska workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while actively seeking new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Nebraska Investment Finance Authority (NIFA) - Housing Programs
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgage programs, down payment assistance, rental assistance, and Low-Income Housing Tax Credits across Nebraska.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Legal Aid of Nebraska
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Nebraskans statewide in areas including housing, family law, consumer protection, public benefits, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Nebraska residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Nebraska Workforce Development - American Job Centers
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, and employment resources at locations across Nebraska.
eligibilitySummary: Any Nebraska resident seeking employment or career development services

name: Nebraska LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible Nebraska households pay heating and cooling bills through the Department of Health and Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Nebraska 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting Nebraskans with local health and human services resources available around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any Nebraska resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Food Bank of Lincoln
description: Feeding America affiliate distributing food to partner agencies and programs serving low-income individuals and families across southeast Nebraska.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in southeast Nebraska

name: Food Bank for the Heartland
description: Feeding America affiliate serving eastern Nebraska and western Iowa, distributing millions of meals through a network of partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in eastern Nebraska

name: Nebraska Department of Veterans' Affairs
description: Assists Nebraska veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, education, employment, and access to the state veterans homes.
eligibilitySummary: Nebraska veterans and their families

name: Nebraska Family Helpline
description: Statewide crisis helpline for families providing support, crisis intervention, and connections to local services for parents and caregivers experiencing stress.
eligibilitySummary: Any Nebraska parent or caregiver experiencing family crisis or stress

name: Nebraska Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working Nebraska families afford quality childcare for their children through the Title XX program.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children under 13 needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 130% of federal poverty level at initial eligibility

name: Nebraska WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, and health referrals for eligible women and young children.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Nebraska ADC / TANF Cash Assistance
description: Aid to Dependent Children providing temporary cash assistance and support services to very low-income families with dependent children in Nebraska.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below ADC payment standard for family size

name: Metro Transit - Omaha Public Transportation
description: Public bus system serving the Omaha metropolitan area with fixed routes and MOBY paratransit providing affordable transportation options.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Omaha metro area; reduced fare for seniors, disabled, and Medicare cardholders

name: StarTran - Lincoln Public Transit
description: Public bus system serving the Lincoln metropolitan area with fixed routes and HandiVan paratransit services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Lincoln area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: Nebraska Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income Nebraska households to reduce utility costs and improve home comfort and safety.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Nebraska Adult Education
description: Free adult education services including GED preparation, English language classes, and workforce readiness training offered statewide through community partners.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: Nebraska Domestic Violence / Sexual Assault Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence or sexual assault in Nebraska

name: Omaha Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Omaha metropolitan area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Omaha area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income for public housing; 50% for vouchers

name: Lincoln Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families, seniors, and disabled individuals in the Lincoln metropolitan area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Lincoln area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income

name: Nebraska Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides employment-related services for Nebraskans with disabilities including vocational counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Nebraska residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: Nebraska CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides health coverage for uninsured children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 218% of federal poverty level

name: Nebraska Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Federal health insurance marketplace where Nebraskans can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium tax credits.
eligibilitySummary: Nebraskans without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL

name: Nebraska Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Nebraskans plus the state's additional Nebraska Telephone Assistance Program.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: United Way of the Midlands - Omaha Area Services
description: Connects Omaha area residents to funded partner agencies providing assistance with basic needs, education, financial stability, and health services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Omaha metro area seeking assistance or community resources

name: Nebraska Department of Education - Special Education
description: Coordinates special education services and early intervention programs for children with disabilities from birth through age 21 across Nebraska.
eligibilitySummary: Children and youth with disabilities from birth through age 21

name: Nebraska Aging and Disability Resource Center
description: One-stop resource center connecting seniors and people with disabilities to long-term services and supports including home care, meals, transportation, and benefits counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Nebraska residents age 60 and older and individuals with disabilities

name: OneWorld Community Health Centers - Omaha
description: Federally qualified health center in Omaha providing affordable primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services on a sliding fee scale.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Omaha area; sliding fee scale for uninsured patients
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on income and family size
hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM CT; some evening hours

name: Nebraska Commission on Latino-Americans
description: State commission connecting Nebraska's Latino community with resources, advocacy, and services including translation, education, and workforce programs.
eligibilitySummary: Latino and Hispanic residents of Nebraska
